column10: https://images-na.ssl-images-amazon.com/images/G/01/x-locale/common/grey-pixel.gif"ASUS Vivobook 15, 13th Gen, Intel Core i3-1315U, 8GB RAM,512GB SSD,15.6" (39.62 cms), FHD, Windows 11,MS Office,Backlit Keyboard,Silver,1.7 kg, X1504VA-NJ320WS, Thin & Light Laptop
column18: I’ve been using the ASUS Vivobook 15 (Intel Core i3-1315U, 13th Gen) for a while now, and it’s a solid choice for students, office work, and light multitasking.  Pros:  Performance: The 13th Gen i3-1315U with 8GB RAM and 512GB SSD makes everyday tasks like browsing, MS Office, and light editing smooth.  Design & Build: Slim, lightweight (1.7 kg), and easy to carry. The silver finish gives it a premium look.  Display: The 15.6" FHD screen is crisp, and bezels are slim for a modern feel.  Keyboard: Backlit keyboard is a big plus for low-light work.  Extras: Windows 11 & MS Office pre-installed save setup time.  Cons:  Not ideal for heavy gaming or high-end video editing.  Battery life is average—around 4-5 hours with moderate use.  RAM is non-upgradeable, which could limit future performance needs.  Verdict: If you need a thin, light, and stylish laptop for office, studies, or light creative work, this Vivobook offers great value for the price. Just don’t expect it to handle very heavy workloads or gaming.  I bought this laptop as a data analytics beginner, and it’s perfect for practicing on small datasets....

column9: I’ve been using the OnePlus Nord CE6 as a heavy user, and honestly, this phone has impressed me a lot in daily usage. The UI is super smooth, clean, and optimized really well. Everything feels fast and fluid.  The BGMI experience is very, very impressive. Initially, I faced some issues, but after turning OFF the Touch Reflex Chip option, the gameplay became much smoother and responsive. I play BGMI around 2 hours daily, and the performance is excellent for long gaming sessions.  Battery backup is also good enough for heavy usage. However, one complaint I have is the charging time. It takes around 1 hour 15–20 minutes to fully charge, which feels a bit high compared to expectations.  Talking about the camera, it surprised me a lot. My previous phone was the Realme GT 6T, and honestly, I found the camera quality on this phone better in many situations.  There are some small cons too. For example, WhatsApp filters are currently not working properly, but I think OnePlus will fix this in future updates.  Please don’t get influenced by negative comments saying this phone is not up to the mark. For day-to-day life, gaming, multitasking, and overall usage, this phone is really good. I’m a software engineer and a heavy user, and I’m fully impressed with this device.

column1: OnePlus Nord CE6 | 8GB+256GB | Fresh Blue | Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 | Segment's Fastest Touch Response | 8000mAh Battery | 144Hz 1.5K AMOLED Display | 50MP Main + 32MP Selfie 4K Cameras | IP66,68,69,69K
column9: 1. Got this phone at 31000(at 2000 bank discount). Do not buy it more then this, wait for some offers or sale.  2. Looks - By touch, look and feel phone is very good. It will give you a expensive one plus phone's feeling (⭐ ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ )  3. Camera - Camera is average. You can click Good picture but if you are a camera guy and expecting a much better camera then you will be disappointed here, else camera is decent. (⭐ ⭐⭐)  4. Battery - This phone has a massive battery of 8000 mah with a 80 watts fast charger. With full charge it can last approx 2 days in normal usage. Charger could be improved as it is having 8000 battery it takes approx 1.5 hours to full charge. It should have 100 watts charger as one plus giving it in its expensive phones, Else battery is best and charging speed is also good. (⭐ ⭐ ⭐⭐)  5. OS update - Measure draw back of this phone is OS update. This phone will get OS update only for 2 years, means this phone is coming with Android 16 and will go till Android 18 only. If you are looking for a phone which you want to use for more then 3-4 years then this phone is not for you. (⭐)  6. Network connectivity - this phone has 5G++ network connectivity and will give you absolutely great 5G speed. (Star (⭐⭐⭐⭐)  7. Colours - This phone comes in 3 colours. My recommendation is go with fresh blue colour instead of black. Fresh blue looks absolutely premium, you will surely not regret it.(⭐ ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ ⭐- for fresh Blue colour)  8. Final recommendation - This phone is good if you need larger battery life, smart looks, larger flat premium display and a decent camera. If you are a pro gammer or a person who needs a camera phone only then this phone is not for you. My recommendation is you can go with this phone it will give you a premium one plus phone's feeling.

Display : 10/10 A great Amoled Screen with uniform bezels. Great screen to body ratio. Colors are nice.  Durability : has a polycarbonate back with aluminium on the side. Feels sturdy and light weight despite the fact that it houses a massive 7100 mAh battery  Speaking of batteries: Battery : 10/10 Easily a 2 day battery phone, even on heavy usage. column9: I recently switched to the OnePlus Nord CE6 after a brief stint with the Nothing Phone 3A Pro (and having unluckily lost my Nord CE4 before that). While the Nothing phone was aesthetically brilliant with great cameras, it heavily missed the mark on daily battery endurance and charging speed for my usage. Moving back to this OnePlus device has been an absolute game-changer.  Here is my breakdown after putting the device through its paces (According to my experiences and usage patterns) :  🔋 Battery Life: Elite Tier Endurance The battery performance on this phone is a literal joke—in the best way possible. On day one alone (while the phone was still indexing and setting up accounts), I hit an unbelievable 8 hours and 31 minutes of Screen-On Time (SOT), and I still had 26% battery remaining! The system estimated another 9 hours of standby on Balanced Mode. The overnight idle drain is practically flat. You can easily clear a 24-hour cycle and push past 10+ hours of heavy SOT before needing a plug.  ⚡ Charging: The Return of True Fast Charging Having to charge my previous 5000 mAh phone twice every 36 hours was becoming a chore. Getting back to the legendary high-speed SUPERVOOC charging ecosystem feels incredibly liberating. Top-ups are ridiculously quick, giving you absolute peace of mind without having to constantly hunt for a wall socket.  📱 Software & Convenience Features Coming back to OxygenOS feels highly optimized. Little daily conveniences work perfectly here. For instance, taking a scrolling screenshot happens smoothly in a single action, unlike other custom UIs that clunkily force you to take multiple manual captures just to stitch an image together. The system launcher and background management are fluid and snappy out of the box.  📦 Storage Efficiency (8GB/128GB Variant) While I am stepping down to 128 GB after using 256 GB phones for a few years, managing it is completely effortless if you manage your data dynamically. With streaming being so seamless and offloading data to a laptop being a breeze, 128 GB is more than enough for current essential usage and apps, especially if you aren’t heavily into hoarding photography.  Final Verdict: If you value raw battery longevity, minimal idle drain, lightning-fast charging speeds, and a reliable, snappy daily user experience, the OnePlus Nord CE6 is an absolute home run. Highly recommended!  Shall update after a month with a more specific review.

column9: If you are looking for a reliable, lag-free daily horse that completely eliminates battery anxiety, the OnePlus Nord CE6 (256GB) is phenomenal. While I don't use it for gaming, everyday apps run incredibly smooth and multitasking is entirely fluid on OxygenOS. The display is gorgeous, and the battery life is absolutely next-level—easily delivering a massive 11 to 13 hours of active Screen-On Time (SOT), which comfortably stretches into nearly 1½ days of regular use on a single charge. 80W charger & bypass charging ensure smooth & cold charging from 20 to 90 percent within hour. For the price point, the camera is also highly capable, snapping sharp and clear daylight photos without any hassle. I love gray color. Sound quality is decent. ​Switching to this phone highlights just how deeply flawed my previous S22 was. The S22 was a total nightmare to live with—its abysmal battery would barely scrape together 3.5 hours of screen-on time, meaning it constantly died before the workday even ended. It also ran uncomfortably hot during basic tasks like simple video calls, causing frequent interface lag. Moving from the constant battery anxiety of the S22 to the cold, multi-day endurance of this Nord CE6 feels like a massive relief. Only best thing it had was 3 set of exceptional cameras which I miss sometime. But I rarely use those feature now.  Finally, I'd Highly recommend Nord CE6 for zero-hassle everyday performance!

column1: insta360 X3 Action Camera, 1/2" Sensor, 5.7K 360 Capture, Optical Zoom 3X, 72 MP 360 Photo, 4K Single Lens Mode, Black
column9: Click to play video I was intrigued by this camera ever since I saw the promo videos over the internet. I was planning to buy a mirrorless initially for my travel photography, but when I saw the capabilities of a 360 cam, I decided to add this to my camera kit. I am writing this review after 2 weeks of using the cam.  Pros- Form factor- Small and light. This can be easily mounted and used for capturing a wide variety of activities. Stability- Insane, the horizon lock and target lock features are superb. Have not captured a blurry shot yet on this device. Picture quality- Amazing, works really well in well lighted situations. You cannot pixel peep on the images as this is a action cam first and not a high end dslr. I am yet to use this in a night time situation. Video quality- Superb, captures at 5.7k and 4k with frame rates between 30 to 60 fps. Neatly stitches out the selfie stick in 360 videos and Me mode, looks like a floating camera in the final shots. Display- Great addition of the large touch screen. But you will have to pump brightness to full while using in broad daylight. Modes- Comes with a bunch of different modes in both 360 and 180. It will keep you pretty busy so they have added a quick setting button. Software- Connecting the camera to the phone was a breeze. The app is well designed, comes with tutorials for different types of content. The cam connects using both BT and Wifi, so you can use your phone as another screen. Viewing and editing content is also good.  Cons- Durability- Bulging lens on both sides so getting protection in form of lens guard and camera cover is a must. This is not rugged like a GoPro and requires careful handling. There is a claimed water resistance but I personally will not expose this to water as I do not trust the capabilities in this area. Heating- The first time I used the cam, I noticed that it was getting very warm. 2 firmware updates later, heating has definitely gone down. Have noticed the heating more when camera is being used with mobile connection. The dual connection of WiFI and BT seems to contribute. Battery life- Decent, but goes down if you are using with mobile connection. You will get a longer battery life if you rely on the cam's display alone. Charging is quick due to USB C type connection.  User friendly- There is a steep learning curve here. Using the camera to capture content is easy but editing 360 degree videos is a different ball game. The phone app has limitations so you cannot process 4k videos on that. You have to use the computer software which still needs some working in the area of user friendliness. The software has AI features which makes extracting images and videos easy but you will have to look into some tutorials to understand the full workflow correctly and get the maximum out of the camera. We are used to dealing with flat files upto now, so this is definitely a different ball game all together.  That said, I do not feel it is a difficult skill to master. After 2 weeks of use, I feel that I am learning more about the camera and its capabilities. The learning curve makes it more exciting to spend time on this and master the various capture and editing skills. This is not for people who like point, shoot and upload. This is for the ones who are willing to go the extra step to learn and use creativity to create pics and videos.

column9: Click to play video I brought this with few concerns, that the quality of the output would meet my expectations. But I'm really surprised with this. This is just mind blowing. You can literally do crazy shots and actions with this tiny creature. More over the quality of the output, you may end up with having lot of fun with this guy ! The output stability for video is much appreciated.  After all if you are a bit caring person, you might also think to make this guy more safe while shooting. His lens are more protruded outwards, which makes more vulnerable for damages or scratches. I suggest to put some lens guard, so that we can sit with peace of mind.  Battery backup is also fine, as it is small and doing it's job well. You can expect 2-3 hours max if you are using this heavily. And the charging hour took almost 60-90 minutes for me from 20% to 100%.  I've gone through many reviews in amazon, where few are got exhausted with the output quality. But if you are good at playing with manual mode operations, that would resolve the issue to an extent in low light conditions. Also, this guy gets heated up a bit more. This may depends on our room condition as here it is very hot these days.  If you are interested in this product, be in mind that you won't get any accessories. The X3 package includes, X3 Camera with battery, Type-C Charging cable (No adapter), Pouch with a lens cleaning cloth and few documentations with a sticker of insta 360.  You have to purchase the stick, and all other action accessories seperately which may cost more. But yes, that won't make you disappointed indeed. 😇

column9: I recieved the X3 today havent used it to take pictures of videos yet. However i am here to express shortcomings i see. A charger should be included in the package it required 5V 3A and all my mobilephone have less Amp. A memory card also should be included since it doesnt upgrade without one nor has internal memory. I got the camera but have ti wait until i get other stuff.  Lens pops out and only while handling for the 1st time i have a fear id scratch the lens cover. Absolutely no protection for the lens at least the rear one must be protected somehow. How do i even place the camera on the table??  The side grip could have been a non slippery silicon feel rather than hard plastic with knurled surface.  The product is heavier than my phone or a small digital camera.  Without using the product, above areas i feel should see improvement.  So on day 3 when i fully setup the camera with the bike mount, memory card and tried some shooting modes, its a fun thing but somehow i feel clarity is an issue. I have used it mostly during the night may be thats one reason.  Another very important suggestion for the manufacturer is the 1/8" insert to fit the stick or mount on the stand, must be replaceable otherwise the camera will be on no use.

column9: I’ve been using these ASIAN Moscow-12 sneakers for a couple of weeks now, and honestly, they’re pretty good for the price.  First thing I noticed is how lightweight they are. I was expecting them to feel a bit bulky since they’re mid-top, but they’re actually really comfortable for everyday wear. The synthetic upper looks decent and gives a clean, minimal white sneaker vibe that goes with almost anything—jeans, joggers, even casual trousers.  Comfort-wise, they’re nice for regular use like walking around, going to college, or casual outings. I wouldn’t say they’re meant for heavy sports or long hours of standing, but for normal daily wear, they do the job well. The cushioning is okay—not super soft, but not hard either.  One small downside is that since they’re white, they get dirty pretty quickly, so you’ll need to clean them often to keep them looking fresh. Also, the fit was slightly snug at first, but it got better after a couple of uses.  Overall, I think these are a solid budget-friendly option if you’re looking for stylish, lightweight sneakers for casual use. Not premium, but definitely worth it for the price 👍
